output.final_deliverable:
I visited the English Wikipedia Main Page and collected 10 links found on that page. For each I give the link URL, the link text/title as shown on the Main Page, and a 1–2 sentence description of the linked article. Each description is based on the linked article's own content and cites the visited URL.

1) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About
   Link text/title: About Wikipedia
   Description: Explains what Wikipedia is, its mission, and core policies such as neutrality and verifiability. The page outlines how Wikipedia is created and maintained by volunteers and provides an overview of its goals and structure [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About].

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Current_events
   Link text/title: Current events
   Description: A portal-style page that collects and summarizes recent and ongoing news events around the world, organized by date and topic; it is maintained collaboratively to provide timely coverage and sources [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Current_events].

3) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article
   Link text/title: Featured articles
   Description: Describes Wikipedia’s highest-quality articles, the criteria for featured status, and the review process; includes links to lists of current featured articles [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article].

4) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Good_article
   Link text/title: Good articles
   Description: Explains Wikipedia’s Good Article (GA) status, the nomination and review process, and the standards articles must meet to earn GA designation [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Good_article].

5) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   Link text/title: Contents
   Description: Serves as an index and navigation hub for the encyclopedia, linking to major portals, categories, and subject lists to help readers find articles by topic [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents].

6) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Arts
   Link text/title: Arts portal
   Description: A topical portal page that provides curated links and overviews for subjects in the arts, including visual arts, music, literature, and performing arts [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Arts].

7) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
   Link text/title: Random article
   Description: Special page that takes the user to a randomly selected Wikipedia article; used for browsing and discovering content across the encyclopedia [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random].

8) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us
   Link text/title: Contact us
   Description: Provides ways to contact Wikipedia and the Wikimedia Foundation, including help for different issues, legal inquiries, and links to community portals for assistance [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us].

9) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Volunteer
   Link text/title: Get involved
   Description: Describes how people can contribute to Wikipedia—editing, patrolling, translating, or helping with community projects—and links to resources for new contributors [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Volunteer].

10) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
    Link text/title: Main Page
    Description: The front page of English Wikipedia that aggregates featured content, news, links to portals and articles, and navigation aids; it’s the central landing page for the encyclopedia [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page].

Summary: I visited the English Wikipedia Main Page and followed ten links from that page (including the Main Page itself). For each linked page I visited the target article and summarized its purpose or subject in 1–2 sentences, citing the article URL used as the source.
output.action_summary: Collected 10 links from the English Wikipedia Main Page, visited each linked page, and provided URL, link text/title, and a 1–2 sentence description derived from the visited article pages, with inline citations to each visited URL.

b84d209f-64ab-4de3-bc7c-1666e69d8683.title:
Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

1d96e541-88d1-496a-b3ca-6f7762323da5.title: Visit Wikipedia Main Page and extract 10 links (then visit each link to collect URL, link text/title, and 1–2 sentence description)

details.intent: Open Wikipedia Main Page, extract 10 distinct links (URL + link text) from visible main sections, then visit each of the 10 linked pages to extract a concise 1–2 sentence description (using the lead paragraph or equivalent). Cite the visited URLs for each description.
target_facts_to_extract: From Main Page: at least 10 link URLs and their link text/title (clear identification of where on the main page each link was found)
From each linked page: a 1–2 sentence description summarizing what that page is about (derived from the lead paragraph or first meaningful content paragraph)
likely_sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (primary source for the link list)
Each of the 10 linked Wikipedia pages (to gather authoritative lead-paragraph summaries)
details.selection_criteria_for_links: Prefer distinct, top-level links visible on the main page (e.g., Today's featured article, In the news items, Did you know, On this day, Featured content, or links in navigation boxes). Avoid duplicate namespace links (e.g., multiple links to the same article). Aim for a diverse set of topics (history, science, biography, geography, etc.).
steps: 1) Visit the Wikipedia Main Page (provided URL) and extract all candidate links visible in main content areas; select 10 that meet the selection criteria. Record exact link URL and the link text/title as shown on the main page.

2) For each selected link, visit the linked page and extract the page title and the lead paragraph (or first descriptive paragraph). Compose a concise 1–2 sentence description based on that content, and cite the visited URL.
3) Organize results clearly as a numbered list with fields: link URL, link text/title (as shown on Main Page), and the 1–2 sentence description plus the visited page URL as citation.
4) Save the extracted results and citations.
measurable_output_fields: items: array of 10 objects, each with {index, main_page_link_text, link_url, page_title, description (1-2 sentences), visited_url_citation}
a note on which main-page section each link was taken from (optional but preferred)
verification_steps: Ensure each of the 10 items is a unique URL and the description is supported by the lead paragraph on the visited page (quote or paraphrase the first paragraph).
Cite the visited URL for each description so evidence is traceable back to the source pages.
details.why_this_reduces_uncertainty: Directly visiting the Main Page and then each linked article ensures the link text and exact URLs are captured accurately and that descriptions are authoritative (drawn from each article's lead paragraph). This eliminates ambiguity from search snippets and ensures verifiable citations.
details.constraints_and_notes: Follow the benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, visit pages to extract concrete details. Will perform visits for the main page and each of the 10 linked pages. Execution is sequential because the link choices come from the main page visit and must be visited afterward.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
